sections.title: Constraint Inventory
sections.content:
Seven constraints define the operational boundary of the ZOLLERN Steel Profiles capability platform. Each constraint is classified by type, severity, and whether it can be mitigated through investment or process change.

| # | Constraint | Type | Severity | Mitigable | Mitigation Cost | Source |
|---|-----------|------|----------|-----------|----------------|--------|
| C1 | 2D cross-section only — cannot produce 3D-varying geometry along length | Physics | HARD | NO | N/A — fundamental process limitation | INSTRUCTIONS.md |
| C2 | Minimum economic batch: ~100 kg (cold drawing), ~500 kg (hot rolling) | Economic | MEDIUM | PARTIAL | Faster die changes reduce threshold ~20% | INSTRUCTIONS.md |
| C3 | Custom tooling lead time: 6-12 weeks for new die/roll design | Temporal | MEDIUM | PARTIAL | EUR 50-100K for in-house die shop upgrade | INSTRUCTIONS.md |
| C4 | Cold drawing limited to ~2,500 mm2 cross-section | Physics | HARD | NO | Machine capacity limit; hot rolling for larger | INSTRUCTIONS.md |
| C5 | Induction hardening requires >= 0.35% C content | Material | HARD | NO | Metallurgical requirement; cannot harden low-C steels | INSTRUCTIONS.md |
| C6 | Steel and stainless steel only — no aluminum, titanium, or non-ferrous | Material | HARD | NO | Different metallurgy requires entirely different equipment | INSTRUCTIONS.md |
| C7 | Maximum single-piece weight limited by handling equipment | Equipment | MEDIUM | YES | Crane/conveyor upgrade EUR 200-500K | INSTRUCTIONS.md |

sections.title: Constraint Classification Matrix
sections.content:
| Constraint | Eliminates Market? | Limits Volume? | Increases Cost? | Reduces Competitiveness? |
|-----------|-------------------|---------------|----------------|------------------------|
| C1 (2D only) | YES — excludes 3D parts | No | No | No |
| C2 (Min batch) | YES — excludes prototyping | Yes — small orders uneconomic | Yes — setup cost spread | Vs. machining for <100kg |
| C3 (Tooling lead) | No | No | Yes — upfront investment | Vs. machining (immediate) |
| C4 (Max cold draw) | No — hot rolling for larger | Limits process choice | No | Minor — hot rolling compensates |
| C5 (Hardening C%) | No — material selection | No | No | Excludes low-C hardened apps |
| C6 (Steel only) | YES — excludes non-ferrous | No | No | Major — no lightweight metals |
| C7 (Max weight) | Rarely | Limits piece length | No | Edge cases only |

sections.title: Market Exclusion Filter
sections.content:
These constraints create a clear exclusion filter for market screening:

| Exclusion Rule | Constraint | Markets Excluded | Example |
|---------------|-----------|-----------------|---------|
| Requires 3D geometry variation | C1 | Turbine blades, crankshafts, complex forgings | Aerospace turbine components |
| Requires non-ferrous metal | C6 | Aluminum extrusions, copper busbars, titanium implants | Architectural aluminum, electronics |
| Requires single-piece production | C2 | One-off prototyping, art/sculpture, custom brackets | R&D prototype services |
| Requires ultra-large cross-section + tight tolerance | C4 | Large precision cold-finished sections | Heavy marine shafting |
| Requires surface hardening on low-C steel | C5 | Case-hardened decorative parts in mild steel | Certain fastener applications |

sections.title: Constraint Interaction Effects
sections.content:
Some constraints interact to create compound limitations:

| Interaction | Constraints | Combined Effect |
|------------|------------|----------------|
| Large + precise | C4 + tolerance | Cross-sections >2,500 mm2 limited to hot rolling tolerances (+/-0.5mm) |
| Fast + custom | C3 + C2 | New market entry requires 6-12 week tooling + minimum volume commitment |
| Hard + light | C5 + C6 | Cannot offer hardened lightweight profiles (aluminum or low-C steel) |

sections.title: Strategic Implications
sections.content:
1. **C1 (2D) and C6 (steel only) are the hardest filters** — they eliminate entire application domains with no workaround
2. **C2 (min batch) and C3 (tooling lead) are the main commercial barriers** — they require customer commitment before first delivery, making sample/trial phases expensive
3. **C3 mitigation is highest-ROI** — investing in faster tooling iteration (3D-printed die prototypes, simulation-based roll design) could cut lead time to 3-4 weeks and dramatically improve market entry speed
4. **C7 is rarely binding** — most profile applications use bar lengths under 6m and weights under 100kg/piece

sections.content:
```json
{
  "constraints": [
    {
      "id": "C1",
      "name": "2D cross-section only",

    },
    {

      "type": "material",
      "severity": "hard",
      "mitigable": false,

      "excluded_markets": ["aluminum_extrusions", "copper_busbars", "titanium_implants"]

    {
      "id": "C7",
```

---

*Sources: INSTRUCTIONS.md constraint list, [ZOLLERN Steel Profiles technical data](https://www.zollern.com/en/steel-profiles/), [ASM Handbook — Drawing and Extrusion](https://www.asminternational.org/)*
